title: Breaking: Jack TEF to Replace Koopman Until the Canadian GP
meta: Published: 5 April 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Jack TEF will return to the cockpit for Williams.
body:
GROVE, UK — Williams Team Principal Jack TEF will return to Formula 1 as a driver, replacing the injured Lucas Koopman for the next three race weekends.

The announcement, made on Saturday morning, confirms TEF will occupy the team's second car for the Bahrain, Saudi Arabian, and Miami Grands Prix. He is expected to vacate the seat when Koopman returns — provisionally at the Canadian Grand Prix on 14 June.

Koopman suffered a back injury in a lap‑one crash at the Japanese Grand Prix. The team initially named Formula 2 title contender Hamish Porter as his replacement, but have now reversed that decision. Porter will continue his F2 championship campaign with Van Amersfoort Racing, where he currently sits second in the standings.

TEF, 20, scored 201 points in the 2025 season, finishing third in the drivers' championship before stepping away from the cockpit to lead Williams. He has not competed in a grand prix since the 2025 Abu Dhabi finale.

His return marks the first time in the modern era that a sitting team principal will race for their own team in Formula 1.

No other active team boss has taken a race seat since the championship began in 1950.

TEF will partner Eren Aygen, who finished second in Japan and sits third in the drivers' championship with 34 points. Williams are currently fifth in the constructors' standings with 37 points, 27 behind leaders Ferrari.

The decision was driven by multiple factors. Koopman's recovery timeline is uncertain, and Williams did not want to rush his return. The team also faced a dilemma with Porter: three weekends away from F2 could derail his title challenge. With TEF already embedded in the team's engineering and operational structure, the internal option emerged as the most practical.

“This is not a decision anyone takes lightly,” a Williams spokesperson said. “Jack knows the car, the procedures, and the pressures. With Lucas needing time and Hamish fighting for an F2 crown, this was the right call for the team.”

TEF has remained race‑fit through simulator work and recently completed a demonstration run in the 1992 Williams FW14B at a private test session. He holds a valid FIA Super Licence.

The paddock reaction has been one of shock. No active team principal has driven in a grand prix since the 1950s, when owner‑drivers were common. The move places TEF in an unprecedented position: he will manage race strategy, driver briefings, and team operations while also stepping into the cockpit on Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays.

Williams have not disclosed how team leadership will function during race weekends. Deputy team principal duties are expected to be shared among senior engineering staff, though no formal interim appointment has been announced.

Koopman is expected to travel to Bahrain as an observer. The Dutch rookie, who qualified sixth and finished 17th in his debut in Australia and finished 13th in China, had shown flashes of pace before his accident. His recovery will be monitored closely ahead of the Canadian round.

TEF's three‑race stint includes one sprint weekend (Saudi Arabia) and two standard grands prix. The circuits — Bahrain International, Jeddah Street Circuit, and Miami International Autodrome — represent a mix of high‑speed, technical, and street layouts. TEF has raced on all three previously, winning in Miami in 2025.

The first practice session in Bahrain is scheduled for 10 April.

What it means: A sitting team principal will race in Formula 1 for the first time in the modern era. Williams take a calculated risk, exchanging a disrupted F2 title campaign for an experienced, race‑winning driver who already knows every member of the team. The move will test the team's operational structure, but it also gives Williams a proven points‑scorer alongside Aygen for three critical rounds. No other team on the grid has ever faced this scenario.

Last updated: 10:45 BST, 5 April 2026

[More to follow]
